learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an improved understanding of career opportunities within the field of study
Demonstrate an ability to relate theoretical knowledge to practice
Demonstrate an ability to critically evaluate your knowledge and skills in the process of applying them in a professional work environment
Demonstrate an ability to comprehend and integrate with accepted workplace practices and policies as they relate to the field of study

assessments.task_description:
Learning Contract to be negotiated with Placement Supervisor during first week of placement, and must incorporate learnings from the Orientation Workshop (compulsory attendance).

Risk Assessment to be undertaken to identify hazards associated with placement activities during the first week of placement.

Templates available on Blackboard under Learning Resources. Both the Learning Contract and Risk Assessment must be endorsed by the Placement Supervisor prior to submission.

Students will advise their due date (based on placement start date) via InPlace.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit your Learning Contract and Risk Assesment through the course Turnitin submission point in Blackboard. Before submitting your assessment item to Turnitin please name your file in the following way. FAMILY NAME_GivenName_StudentID_CourseCode_AssignmentName For example: Bob Smith (ID: 54329876) would name his ABCD1234 essay 1 in the following way. SMITH_Bob_54329876_ABCD1234_Essay1
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item, then 10% of the maximum possible mark for the assessment item (assessment ‘marked from’ value) will be deducted as a late penalty for every day (or part day) late after the due date. For example, if you submit your assignment 1 hour late, you will be penalised 10%; if your assignment is 24.5 hours late, you will be penalised 20% (because it is late by one 24-hour period plus part of another 24-hour period). 10% will be deducted per day for up to 7 calendar days, at which point your submission will receive a mark of zero (0) unless an extension has been approved.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Final video reflection due date 6/08/2026 3:00 pm

Progressive reflections due after every 40 hours of placement. Final reflection due one week after after post-placement workshops.
assessments.task_description:
You will submit four (4) reflections for this assessment item:

  • three (3) progressive written reflections to be submitted during your placement, evenly spaced over the 120 hours; and
  • one (1) final video reflection submitted one (1) week after the post-placement workshops.


Progressive reflections

Using the SEAL template on blackboard, you will periodically reflect on events and activities that occur during your placement. Training on using the SEAL template will be given in the orientation workshop.

  • Reflective Practice 1 is for situations that occur in the first block of 40 hours of placement.
  • Reflective Practice 2 is for situations that occur in the second block of 40 hours of placement.
  • Reflective Practice 3 is for situations that occur in the final block of 40 hours of placement.

E.g. If your placement period is six weeks, you will submit your first progressive reflection after two weeks of placement are completed, your second reflection after four weeks, and your third upon completion of the placement. Students will calculate their own schedule for submitting progressive Reflective Practice assessments and enter the details on InPlace. Reflective Practice 3 must be submitted in time for marking/feedback to occur in preparation for the post-placement workshops.

Final reflection

You will prepare a 3-5 minute video, speaking to camera, for your final reflection, drawing on previous reflective practice. Your final reflection must incorporate learnings from successful completion of the Post-Placement workshop activities (compulsory attendance).

Your video will be marked with reference tot he published criteria available on Blackboard.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.

Before submitting your assessment item to Turnitin please name your file in the following way. FAMILY NAME_GivenName_StudentID_CourseCode_AssignmentName For example: Bob Smith (ID: 54329876) would name his ABCD1234 essay 1 in the following way. SMITH_Bob_54329876_ABCD1234_Essay1
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.weighting: <dd> Pass/Fail. Confirmation of 120 hours and at least 8/20 marks for a Pass. </dd>
assessments.task_description:
Prior to completion of your placement, UQ will send a request via email to your placement supervisor to complete an assessment of your performance in the workplace and to confirm the number of placement hours completed. Supervisors will be encouraged to use the report as an opportunity to conduct a formal feedback session with you prior to the end of your placement.

The report consists of three components.

1. Confirmation of the student's attendance and participation in the placement for a minimum of 120 hours.

2. Assessment of student performance against the following criteria:

  • Written and interpersonal communication
  • Quality of work, knowledge, and problem solving
  • Organisation and work ethic
  • Cooperation and collaboration
  • Attendance and presentation

3. Written feedback on the student's strengths, challenges, and performance on tasks assigned during the placement.

Supervisors will complete the report electronically, and submit directly to UQ. The feedback will be released to students following review by UQ.

A copy of the report your supervisor is asked to complete will be available on Blackboard.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Placement supervisor to submit report directly to UQ.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
27/07/2026 5:00 pm

Slide must be submitted by the due date (48 hours before the first post-placement workshop)

Presentation must be delivered live, in person, at the first post-placement workshop
assessments.task_description:
Students will deliver a three-minute-thesis style presentation, using a single PowerPoint slide, to share details of their experience with the rest of the cohort.

Submit your single presentation slide as a PowerPoint file via the Presentation submission link on Blackboard by the due date listed here.

All students will deliver their presentation live, in-person, during the Post-Placement Presentation Seminar (compulsory attendance - see timetable for details).

The presentation should be written for a general audience (i.e., not too technical) and convey details of the activities undertaken during placement without breaching any confidentiality restrictions of the host organisation.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.

learning_activities.topic:
Pre-placement Workshop

Compulsory orientation workshop for all course participants. This workshop is in-person and will not be offered by Zoom. The workshop is timetabled as follows:

  • Orientation Workshop: Saturday 30 May 2026 (9am-1pm), Room 111, Chamberlain Building (35), St Lucia Campus

In addition to this live, in-person session, students are required to complete the 'Crash Course into Employer Expectations' module and view the 'Health, Safety and Wellness on placement' video prior to commencing placement. Both resources will be found on Blackboard.


Learning outcomes: L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 5 To Week 9(22 Jun - 26 Jul)
learning_activities.topic:
Placement

Students will undertake a minimum of 120 hours placement (maximum 160 hours for unpaid placements) in an approved workplace between these dates: 22 June - 24 July 2026. Note: if placement dates clash with workshop dates, students must seek 'time off' from their placement to attend compulsory workshops.


Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Placement Students will undertake a minimum of 120 hours placement (maximum 160 hours for unpaid placements) in an approved workplace between these dates: 22 June - 24 July 2026. Note: if placement dates clash with workshop dates, students must seek 'time off' from their placement to attend compulsory workshops.

learning_activities.topic:
Post-placement Workshops

Compulsory workshops for all course participants.

Students will deliver a presentation, engage in discussion with peers, practice self-reflection, and undertake self-assessment of employability literacies. There are two timetabled workshops, and all students must attend both sessions:

  • Post-placement Presentation Seminar
  • Post-placement Employability Workshop

See timetable in SI-net for details. These workshops are in person and will not be offered by Zoom.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
